title: People should ride their bikes instead of drive their cars

transcript.text: Hello, this video is, I think people should ride their bikes instead of drive their cars.
The main reason, well, yeah, the main reason, there's lots of main reasons, but I think the main reason people should ride their bikes is health.
When you ride your bike, it's a really good way of exercising.
It works most of the muscles in your body, you can pump yourself as hard as you can, and, you know, you sweat a lot.
And it's a good reason to get, I mean, it's like if you ride your bike instead of drive your car, you're killing two birds with one stone, basically.
Um, you're getting to the destination and you're getting a workout at the same time.
So it's kind of like you're accomplishing all this stuff and, uh, not using a whole lot of time to do it, you know?
Everybody wants to get in shape, everybody wants to lose their belly or their ass.
Bicycling's a perfect way to do that.
Lose your belly, get a tight ass, you win.
Um, I mention this to a lot of people who don't, and they say, oh, you know, I'm not athletic, I don't do that.
And, um, I can understand that it, it can be physically painful to get exercise if you're not used to it.
There's lots of toxins in your body that get released, and as they're releasing it, it's physically not very comfortable.
But the thing is, is after a couple of days, even, just a couple of days of it, you get used to it, and then you get addicted to it.
It's like a drug, you get high off of it.
And, um, I guarantee, if you just give it a try, you'll do it, and you'll love it.
Um, I had a, I was an English teacher in Santiago for five years, and I was, you know, I'd ride my bike from my house to this business,
And then back to my house, or from this class over here to this person's house over there.
And on some of the busier days, I would ride my bike, like, like, I looked at my odometer, it was like 37 miles a day I'm riding my bike.
And it would be, like, periods of, like, a half hour that I'd ride my bike.
As fast as I possibly could, when I wasn't smoking dope.
I'd be going as fast as I could.
And I was getting strong, man.
I was, like, the fastest I've ever been.
And it was awesome, because I didn't have to take any of my own time out of my schedule.
And I was getting around town faster than a car, a lot faster than anybody in a car, because I was running red lights.
Um, I'm not saying you should run red lights on a bike, but, you know, you can't get away with it.
You definitely can do that.
Especially in, you know, countries like that.
Um, so, I guarantee, just give it a try.
It'll get you in shape.
But, now, as far as, um, the, uh, other reasons, um, pollution.
Cars pollute.

And then we'll be able to use that to make plastic, you know, and lots of other reasons.
It's a lot less expensive for you.
Cars are very expensive to maintain.
Um, and the main reason is that you drive them.
If you drive your car, you're going to have to maintain it.
Um, so, it's, uh, definitely a good idea to ride your bike.

Please don't get too close to bikers when you pass them.
Because there are potholes in the road that people swerve to avoid, dead animals that people swerve to avoid.
And if you only give somebody, like, a foot, they could swerve in your way in the last minute, and you could kill somebody.
Um, and you don't really have to be going that fast, too.
Um, and, um, so, please ride your bike.
Um, and, you know, some people say, oh, but I live, like, 10 miles from my work.
